      "human–AI collaboration", "human–AI decision-making", "human–AI readiness / team readiness", "model accuracy", "miscalibrated reliance", "overreliance", "underuse / under-reliance", "appropriate reliance", "onboarding", "calibration of reliance", "governance", "interaction / decision traces", "trust", "explainable AI (XAI)", "contestability", "accountability", "automation bias / AI-induced error", "calibration cards", "regions-of-no-use", "safe levers", "counterfactual examples", "curated failure sets", "mental models of AI reliability", "reliability boundaries", "delegation and autonomy", "Outcome metrics", "Reliance & Interaction metrics", "Safety & Harm metrics", "Learning & Readiness metrics", "team gain", "regret_best / avoidable error", "oracle best accuracy", "error recovery", "error amplification", "accept-on-wrong", "changed-to-wrong (ChangedToWrong)", "ChangedToRight", "override frequency and timing", "reliance slope", "local vs. global update asymmetry", "AI-harm", "AI-help", "near-misses", "governance-in-use signals", "rollback", "escalation", "rule–behavior contradiction", "calibration gap", "stability under distribution shift", "transfer", "retention", "intervention latency", "time-to-calibration", "human accuracy", "AI accuracy", "team accuracy", "missed-help", "correct-ignore", "reliance behavior", "Understand (U–C–I stage)", "Control (U–C–I stage)", "Improve (U–C–I stage)", "governance mechanisms (model cards, audit trails)", "AI-ready"
    ]

  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c1", "name": "human–AI collaboration", "aliases": ["human-AI teaming", "human–AI teams"], "description": "Collaborative decision-making in which AI systems support rather than replace human judgment.", "grounding_span": "safe and effective human–AI collaboration", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c2", "name": "human–AI decision-making", "aliases": ["AI-assisted decision-making"], "description": "Decision-making settings where humans make final decisions after viewing AI advice.", "grounding_span": "human-AI decision-making centered on team readiness", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c3", "name": "human–AI readiness", "aliases": ["team readiness", "AI-ready"], "description": "Whether a human–AI team is prepared to collaborate safely and effectively, treated as a behavioral capability.", "grounding_span": "cumulative research on human–AI readiness", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c4", "name": "model accuracy", "aliases": ["predictive performance"], "description": "Whether a model's prediction matches ground truth; insufficient as sole evaluation target.", "grounding_span": "Many studies emphasize model accuracy", "location": "Section 2.1"},
    {"id": "c5", "name": "miscalibrated reliance", "aliases": [], "description": "Reliance that does not track AI correctness, producing overuse or underuse.", "grounding_span": "many failures arise from miscalibrated reliance", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c6", "name": "overreliance", "aliases": ["overuse"], "description": "Following AI advice when it is wrong.", "grounding_span": "overuse when AI is wrong and underuse when it is helpful", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c7", "name": "underuse", "aliases": ["under-reliance"], "description": "Failing to adopt correct AI advice when it would help.", "grounding_span": "underuse when AI is helpful", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c8", "name": "appropriate reliance", "aliases": [], "description": "Accepting AI when likely correct and overriding when likely wrong.", "grounding_span": "Appropriate Reliance, AI Evaluation Metrics", "location": "Keywords"},
    {"id": "c9", "name": "onboarding", "aliases": ["AI onboarding"], "description": "The process through which users learn to work effectively with AI systems in real decision-making settings; reframed as a measurable learning intervention.", "grounding_span": "reframes onboarding as a measurable learning", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c10", "name": "calibration of reliance", "aliases": ["calibrate reliance"], "description": "Adjusting acceptance/override of AI based on evidence and uncertainty cues.", "grounding_span": "calibrate reliance", "location": "Section 2.2"},
    {"id": "c11", "name": "governance", "aliases": ["governance in use"], "description": "Accountability enacted through everyday interaction (contestation, rollback, escalation, auditing).", "grounding_span": "AI governance as enacted in practice", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "c12", "name": "interaction traces", "aliases": ["decision traces", "interaction logs"], "description": "Observable logs of initial human decisions, AI recommendations, and final decisions from which metrics are computed.", "grounding_span": "operationalizing evaluation through interaction traces rather", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c13", "name": "trust", "aliases": ["self-reported trust", "perceived trust"], "description": "An attitude, often measured via surveys, that poorly predicts actual reliance behavior.", "grounding_span": "model properties or self-reported trust", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c14", "name": "explainable AI (XAI)", "aliases": ["XAI"], "description": "Mechanisms (attributions, examples, counterfactuals, rules, uncertainty) to make model behavior intelligible.", "grounding_span": "explainable AI (XAI) and", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c15", "name": "contestability", "aliases": [], "description": "The user's ability to contest and intervene in AI decisions.", "grounding_span": "contestability, auditing", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c16", "name": "accountability", "aliases": [], "description": "Responsibility enacted under realistic constraints, evidenced through behavior rather than documentation.", "grounding_span": "remain accountable under realistic constraints", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c17", "name": "automation bias", "aliases": ["AI-induced error"], "description": "Users changing initially correct judgments to incorrect ones after seeing AI advice.", "grounding_span": "often referred to as AI-induced error or automation bias", "location": "Section 2.1.1"},
    {"id": "c18", "name": "calibration cards", "aliases": [], "description": "Artifacts that summarize when AI predictions are reliable or unreliable.", "grounding_span": "calibration cards", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c19", "name": "regions-of-no-use", "aliases": [], "description": "Contexts where AI recommendations should not be trusted.", "grounding_span": "regions-of-no-use, contexts where AI recom", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c20", "name": "safe levers", "aliases": [], "description": "User-facing controls allowing bounded intervention in AI behavior with preview, rollback, and audit trails.", "grounding_span": "safe levers, user-facing", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c21", "name": "counterfactual examples", "aliases": [], "description": "Examples revealing how small input changes can flip predictions, used in onboarding.", "grounding_span": "counterfactual examples that reveal how", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c22", "name": "curated failure sets", "aliases": ["failure sets"], "description": "Structured sets of failure cases used to build mental models during onboarding.", "grounding_span": "curated failure sets and counterfactual", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c23", "name": "mental models of AI reliability", "aliases": ["mental models"], "description": "Users' internal models of when AI is reliable, updated locally or globally after failures.", "grounding_span": "revise their broader mental model of AI reliability", "location": "Section 2.3.2"},
    {"id": "c24", "name": "reliability boundaries", "aliases": [], "description": "Knowing when AI is likely correct or incorrect rather than assuming uniform performance.", "grounding_span": "reliability boundaries", "location": "Section 2.2"},
    {"id": "c25", "name": "delegation and autonomy", "aliases": ["selective deferral"], "description": "How responsibility shifts between human and AI under different operating modes.", "grounding_span": "understand delegation and autonomy", "location": "Section 2.2"},
    {"id": "c26", "name": "Outcome metrics", "aliases": ["What happened?"], "description": "Metrics capturing quality of final human–AI decisions beyond raw model correctness.", "grounding_span": "Outcome metrics evaluate decision quality", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c27", "name": "Reliance & Interaction metrics", "aliases": ["How was AI used?"], "description": "Metrics characterizing how AI advice shapes human decisions, operationalizing behavioral calibration.", "grounding_span": "Reliance & Interaction metrics", "location": "Section 2.3.2"},
    {"id": "c28", "name": "Safety & Harm metrics", "aliases": ["What went wrong?"], "description": "Metrics attributing harm to AI influence and governance breakdowns.", "grounding_span": "Safety and Harm metrics identify high-risk collaboration failures", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c29", "name": "Learning & Readiness metrics", "aliases": ["What changed over time?"], "description": "Metrics assessing whether onboarding produces durable, transferable skill.", "grounding_span": "Learning & Readiness metrics: assess whether onboarding", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c30", "name": "team gain", "aliases": ["TeamGain"], "description": "Improvement (or degradation) from human-only or AI-only baselines to the final team decision.", "grounding_span": "team gain and avoidable error", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c31", "name": "regret_best", "aliases": ["avoidable error"], "description": "Proportion of avoidable errors where the team fails despite at least one agent being correct.", "grounding_span": "regret_best", "location": "Section 2.3.1"},
    {"id": "c32", "name": "oracle best accuracy", "aliases": ["oracle upper bound"], "description": "Upper bound on achievable team performance if one always selected the correct agent.", "grounding_span": "Oracle best accuracy", "location": "Section 2.3.1"},
    {"id": "c33", "name": "error recovery", "aliases": [], "description": "Cases where an initially incorrect human decision becomes correct after viewing AI output.", "grounding_span": "error recovery vs. error amplification", "location": "Section 2.3.1"},
    {"id": "c34", "name": "error amplification", "aliases": [], "description": "Cases where a correct initial human decision becomes incorrect due to AI influence.", "grounding_span": "error recovery vs. error amplification", "location": "Section 2.3.1"},
    {"id": "c35", "name": "accept-on-wrong", "aliases": [], "description": "Overreliance on incorrect AI predictions.", "grounding_span": "accept-on-wrong", "location": "Section 2.3.2"},
    {"id": "c36", "name": "ChangedToWrong", "aliases": ["changed-to-wrong"], "description": "Harmful changes from a correct human judgment to an incorrect final decision after seeing AI.", "grounding_span": "changed-to-wrong", "location": "Section 2.3.2"},
    {"id": "c37", "name": "ChangedToRight", "aliases": [], "description": "Beneficial changes from an incorrect to a correct decision.", "grounding_span": "ChangedToRight", "location": "Appendix A.2"},
    {"id": "c38", "name": "override frequency and timing", "aliases": ["override rate + timing"], "description": "How often and how quickly users override AI advice.", "grounding_span": "override frequency and timing, and reliance slope", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c39", "name": "reliance slope", "aliases": [], "description": "Sensitivity of reliance to AI correctness; higher values indicate better calibration.", "grounding_span": "reliance slope", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c40", "name": "local vs. global update asymmetry", "aliases": [], "description": "Whether users treat an AI failure as case-specific or revise their broader model of AI reliability.", "grounding_span": "Local vs. global update asymmetry.", "location": "Appendix A.2"},
    {"id": "c41", "name": "AI-harm", "aliases": ["AI-induced harm"], "description": "Cases where AI causes a correct human decision to become wrong.", "grounding_span": "AI-harm", "location": "Section 2.3.3"},
    {"id": "c42", "name": "AI-help", "aliases": [], "description": "Cases where AI corrects an initially wrong human decision.", "grounding_span": "AI-help: cases where AI corrects", "location": "Appendix A.3"},
    {"id": "c43", "name": "near-misses", "aliases": ["high-risk disagreements"], "description": "High-risk cases where an incorrect AI recommendation was narrowly avoided through human intervention.", "grounding_span": "near-misses", "location": "Section 2.3.3"},
    {"id": "c44", "name": "governance-in-use signals", "aliases": [], "description": "Behavioral evidence of governance: rollback, escalation, and contradiction detection.", "grounding_span": "governance-in-use signals", "location": "Section 2.3.3"},
    {"id": "c45", "name": "rollback", "aliases": ["rollback rate"], "description": "Reversing AI-influenced decisions after review.", "grounding_span": "rollback events", "location": "Section 2.3.3"},
    {"id": "c46", "name": "escalation", "aliases": ["escalation rate"], "description": "Referring cases for human or institutional oversight.", "grounding_span": "escalation actions", "location": "Section 2.3.3"},
    {"id": "c47", "name": "rule–behavior contradiction", "aliases": [], "description": "Violations where required actions (e.g., escalation) are not taken.", "grounding_span": "Rule–behavior contradiction", "location": "Appendix A.3"},
    {"id": "c48", "name": "calibration gap", "aliases": [], "description": "Misalignment between reported confidence and actual correctness.", "grounding_span": "Calibration gap", "location": "Appendix A.4"},
    {"id": "c49", "name": "stability under distribution shift", "aliases": [], "description": "Whether calibration and reliance remain stable as data distribution shifts.", "grounding_span": "under distribution shift", "location": "Section 2.1.3"},
    {"id": "c50", "name": "transfer", "aliases": [], "description": "Consistency of performance or reliance across tasks, datasets, or model versions.", "grounding_span": "Transfer: consistency of performance or reliance across", "location": "Appendix A.4"},
    {"id": "c51", "name": "retention", "aliases": [], "description": "Stability of calibration-related metrics across multiple sessions or time intervals.", "grounding_span": "Retention: stability of calibration-related metrics", "location": "Appendix A.4"},
    {"id": "c52", "name": "intervention latency", "aliases": [], "description": "Average time taken to confirm or override AI, reflecting hesitation and contesting.", "grounding_span": "Intervention latency", "location": "Appendix A.2"},
    {"id": "c53", "name": "time-to-calibration", "aliases": [], "description": "Rolling-window estimate of how quickly stable calibration is reached.", "grounding_span": "time-to-calibration", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c54", "name": "human accuracy", "aliases": ["Acc_h0"], "description": "Proportion of cases correctly solved by the human before seeing AI.", "grounding_span": "Human accuracy", "location": "Appendix A.1"},
    {"id": "c55", "name": "AI accuracy", "aliases": ["Acc_ai"], "description": "Proportion of cases correctly predicted by the AI.", "grounding_span": "AI accuracy", "location": "Appendix A.1"},
    {"id": "c56", "name": "team accuracy", "aliases": ["Acc_team"], "description": "Proportion of cases where the final human–AI decision is correct.", "grounding_span": "Team accuracy", "location": "Appendix A.1"},
    {"id": "c57", "name": "missed-help", "aliases": ["under-reliance"], "description": "Failures to adopt correct AI advice when the human is wrong.", "grounding_span": "Missed-help", "location": "Appendix A.3"},
    {"id": "c58", "name": "correct-ignore", "aliases": [], "description": "Appropriate rejection of incorrect AI advice.", "grounding_span": "Correct-ignore", "location": "Appendix A.3"},
    {"id": "c59", "name": "reliance behavior", "aliases": [], "description": "Situated behavior of deferring to or overriding AI under constraints.", "grounding_span": "reliance behavior, safety signals, and learning over time", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c60", "name": "Understand", "aliases": ["U (U–C–I stage)"], "description": "Stage where users develop mental models of model behavior, boundary conditions, and failure modes.", "grounding_span": "InUnderstand, users develop mental models", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c61", "name": "Control", "aliases": ["C (U–C–I stage)"], "description": "Stage where users learn to calibrate reliance and apply safe interventions.", "grounding_span": "InControl, users", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c62", "name": "Improve", "aliases": ["I (U–C–I stage)"], "description": "Stage where teams iteratively refine collaboration strategies and governance policies.", "grounding_span": "InImprove, teams iteratively refine collaboration strategies", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c63", "name": "governance mechanisms", "aliases": ["model cards", "audit trails"], "description": "Documentation-based mechanisms (model cards, audit trails, policies) that are necessary but insufficient alone.", "grounding_span": "Governance mechanisms (e.g. model cards, audit trails, policies)", "location": "Section 2.3.5"},
    {"id": "c64", "name": "AI-ready", "aliases": ["AI readiness threshold"], "description": "The state in which a user meets behavioral criteria for deployment beyond short-term task performance.", "grounding_span": "When does a user become “AI-ready”?", "location": "Section 1"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d1", "term": "regions-of-no-use", "verbatim_text": "contexts where AI recommendations should not be trusted", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "regions-of-no-use, contexts where AI recom", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "d2", "term": "safe levers", "verbatim_text": "user-facing controls (e.g. rule/threshold edits) that allow bounded intervention in AI behavior with preview, rollback, and audit trails", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "safe levers, user-facing", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "d3", "term": "error recovery", "verbatim_text": "cases where an initially incorrect human decision becomes correct after viewing AI output", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "becomes correct after viewing AI output", "location": "Appendix A.1"},
    {"id": "d4", "term": "error amplification", "verbatim_text": "cases where a correct initial human decision becomes incorrect due to AI influence", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "refers to cases where a correct initial human decision becomes", "location": "Appendix A.1"},
    {"id": "d5", "term": "AI-harm", "verbatim_text": "cases where AI causes a correct human decision to become wrong", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "AI-harm: cases where AI causes a correct human decision", "location": "Appendix A.3"},
    {"id": "d6", "term": "AI-help", "verbatim_text": "cases where AI corrects an initially wrong human decision", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "AI-help: cases where AI corrects an initially wrong human", "location": "Appendix A.3"},
    {"id": "d7", "term": "local updates", "verbatim_text": "isolated overrides or rejections confined to the current case", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "Local updatesare reflected by isolated overrides or rejec", "location": "Appendix A.2"},
    {"id": "d8", "term": "global updates", "verbatim_text": "systematic changes in reliance behavior on subsequent cases", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "as systematic changes in reliance behavior on subsequent cases.", "location": "Appendix A.2"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl1", "claim_text": "Model performance alone is insufficient for safe and effective human–AI collaboration.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "is insufficient for safe and effective human–AI collaboration", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl2", "claim_text": "Trust often poorly predicts reliance behavior.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "trust often poorly predicts", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl3", "claim_text": "Explanations can increase overreliance by providing a false sense of certainty or legitimacy.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "providing a false sense of certainty or legitimacy", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl4", "claim_text": "Explainability and interactive ML techniques do not reliably improve decision quality by default.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "do not reliably improve decision quality", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl5", "claim_text": "Behavioral evidence consistently shows weak alignment between reported trust and actual reliance.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "consistently shows weak alignment between reported trust and", "location": "Section 2.1.2"},
    {"id": "cl6", "claim_text": "High task performance during evaluation does not imply that users are prepared for real-world deployment.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "evaluation does not imply that users are prepared for real-world", "location": "Section 2.1.3"},
    {"id": "cl7", "claim_text": "Even highly accurate systems can degrade team performance when users over-rely on incorrect advice or fail to intervene at critical moments.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "over-rely on incorrect advice or fail to intervene at critical moments", "location": "Section 2.3.5"},
    {"id": "cl8", "claim_text": "Governance mechanisms are necessary but insufficient on their own.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "are necessary but insufficient on their own", "location": "Section 2.3.5"},
    {"id": "cl9", "claim_text": "Progress in human–AI collaboration requires shifting from evaluating models in isolation to evaluating human–AI teams.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "requires shifting from evaluating models in isolation to evaluating", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl10", "claim_text": "Calibration should be treated as a primary evaluation target, not a byproduct of explainability.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "calibration should be treated as aprimaryeval", "location": "Section 2.3.5"},
    {"id": "cl11", "claim_text": "Without measuring onboarding, calibration, and harm, we cannot claim that human–AI systems are ready for real-world collaboration.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "we cannot claim that human–AI systems are ready for real-world", "location": "Section 3"}
  ],

  "frameworks": [
    {"id": "f1", "name": "Four-part taxonomy of evaluation metrics", "owner": "Min Hun Lee", "year": 2026, "grounding_span": "four-part taxonomy of evaluation metrics spanning out", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "f2", "name": "Understand–Control–Improve (U–C–I) lifecycle", "owner": "Min Hun Lee", "year": 2026, "grounding_span": "(U–C–I) lifecycle of human-AI onboarding and collaboration.", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "f3", "name": "Unified, trace-based evaluation framework for human–AI readiness", "owner": "Min Hun Lee", "year": 2026, "grounding_span": "We contribute aunified, traced-based evaluation", "location": "Section 1"}
  ],

    {"grounding_span": "each metric class maps naturally to stages of the\nUnderstand–Control–Improve (U–C–I)onboarding lifecycle", "suggested_edge": "maps_to_stage", "from": "f1", "to": "f2", "note": "The four metric families are mapped to U–C–I lifecycle stages (observable + actionable). No schema edge expresses a taxonomy-family → lifecycle-stage mapping."},
    {"grounding_span": "extending recent work on AI onboarding and explanation-\nsupported learning for clinical decision-making", "suggested_edge": "extends_prior_work", "from": "f2", "to": "lee-2024-improving-onboarding-ai-xai", "note": "The U–C–I framework extends prior AI-onboarding work; extends is restricted to Definition→Definition or Framework→Framework, and the cited works are Documents not Frameworks."},
    {"grounding_span": "we reframe onboarding not as documen-\ntation, demos, or one-off training, but as ameasurable learning\nintervention", "suggested_edge": "reconceptualizes_as", "from": "c9", "to": "c9", "note": "Document reframes the concept 'onboarding' as a measurable learning intervention — a re-definition/reconceptualization relation not expressible with allowed edges."},
    {"grounding_span": "Reliance slope: sensitivity of reliance to AI correctness;\nhigher values indicate better calibration", "suggested_edge": "indicator_of", "from": "c39", "to": "c10", "note": "Reliance slope is an operational indicator of calibration quality; no edge expresses 'metric is an indicator/proxy of construct'."},
    {"grounding_span": "Design action (what you do when it’s bad)", "suggested_edge": "triggers_design_action", "from": "c35", "to": "c19", "note": "Tables 1–2 map bad metric values to design actions (e.g., accept-on-wrong → introduce regions-of-no-use).
